title: Game content extraction 盲盒物品四类内容池重构
problem: The current pilot blind-box model still carries conditional/risky semantics that do not match the tool's capabilities. The tool cannot inspect images, so anchor-based items are invalid. blocked_or_risky should not be a content category. The content model should use four default-safe pools, with the fourth pool redefined as scene_expansion_items.
goals: Replace conditional_items / anchor_required_items concepts with scene_expansion_items.
Remove blocked_or_risky as a content pool and handle risky content through blocked_patterns validation.
Keep core_items, support_items, and visible_small_items as the first three content categories.
Define scene_expansion_items as medium-or-larger, boundary-clear, scene-enriching objects that do not require image anchors.
Preserve the legacy BLIND_BOXES four-column runtime contract for the UI.
Rewrite the three pilot boxes 15 桌面+学习, 16 海底+潜水, and 17 公园+野餐 with the four-pool model.
Add tests that prevent anchor-dependent, thin/string-like, trace-like, transparent/reflective/glowing, and animal-body terms from entering default pools.
non_goals: No image recognition or anchor detection.
No user text trigger parser in this phase.
No full 20-category expansion in the first implementation.
No release packaging/version bump unless requested later.

core_items.definition: The most typical and stable primary objects for the scene.

support_items.definition: Tools, containers, storage, carriers, or functional accessories around core objects.

visible_small_items.definition: Grouped, blocky, or carrier-backed small items with clear boundaries.

scene_expansion_items.definition: Medium-or-larger objects that naturally enrich the scene, add variety, and do not require any image anchor or pre-existing object to be valid.

blocked_patterns.role: Validation/test rules only, not content pools.

acceptance_criteria: Pilot content bundles use exactly four content pools: core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, scene_expansion_items.
conditional_items and blocked_or_risky are no longer candidate pools in the pilot model.
scene_expansion_items contain only medium-or-larger, common, boundary-clear, independently circleable objects that are reasonable from the selected scene category alone.
scene_expansion_items do not require image facts such as visible monitor, whiteboard, umbrella pole, wall hook, or available side space.
blocked_patterns validation prevents thin/string-like, trace-like, transparent/reflective/glowing, animal-body, and micro-detail terms from entering default pools.
Legacy tests for 20 entries, three pilots, box ids 15/16/17, input override, and runtime four-column compatibility continue to pass.
New tests cover the four-pool schema and the removal of blocked_or_risky from content bundles.
recommended_next_step: Run spec-generator on this handoff, then workflow-plan and workflow-execute for the four-pool content refactor.
